name: Harvard College
notes: Rev. Thomas Shepard (I00002) was instrumental in founding Harvard and placing it at Cambridge. Rev. Samuel Shepard (H00008) graduated 1658; Samuel Shepard grandson (H00038) graduated 1685; Col. Roger Newton (H00044) also a graduate.

name: Yale University (College of Connecticut / Yale College)
notes: Co-founded 1701 by a consortium of Congregational ministers including Rev. James Pierpont (H00116) — son of Mary Hooker Pierpont (H00034), daughter of Rev. Thomas Hooker. James Pierpont is a direct 2nd-generation Hooker descendant and one of Yale's primary founders. Yale's founding is inseparable from the Hooker-Pierpont family network. James Pierpont's daughter Sarah Pierpont (HD0418) married Rev. Jonathan Edwards; their grandson Aaron Burr Jr. (H00913) attended Princeton. The Yale connections in this database are tracked separately in the `yale_connections` array (95 entries as of v0.3.25), covering alumni, faculty, and other relationships across the Hooker tree. Primary Hooker connection: H00116 James Pierpont (co-founder), H00034 Mary Hooker Pierpont (his mother). WHITNEY PORTRAIT: The Morse portrait of Eli Whitney (1822) — accession 1827.1, among the earliest formally catalogued works — was donated by George Hoadley (B.A. 1801), husband of Hooker descendant Mary Ann Woolsey Hoadley (HD2075). Whitney had married Hooker descendant Henrietta Frances Edwards (HD2046). Yale Art Gallery: https://artgallery.yale.edu/collections/objects/31 PERCIVAL PAPERS: James Gates Percival (X01936, B.A. 1815, M.D. 1820) — papers held at Yale Beinecke Library (YCAL MSS 703), including correspondence with Emma Hart Willard (H00597) in which both Hooker descendants acknowledged their family connection.
